title: Playground Browser Desk
concept: A browser-chrome redesign of WordPress Playground where Playgrounds are tabs, the WordPress path is a real address bar, and creation, saving, saved management, Site Manager drawers, Blueprints, and transfers are organized around browser-like controls for first-time learners.
focus: browser tabs, address bar, reload, Homepage, WP Admin, save state, and Site Manager controls in the chrome
left navigation plus full-height content instead of modal-first management
route-specific launch drawers for Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, and .zip import
visible save destination, save progress, saved identity, rename, delete, reset, reload, and import consequences
credible Blueprint catalog with filters, dense results, selected detail, inspect, copy, download, and run actions
flows: Choose a launch source from the browser new-window panel, inspect route-specific inputs and constraints, then start or preview the selected Playground
Save a temporary Playground either in browser storage or to a local directory, including progress and resulting saved state
Browse saved and unsaved Playgrounds, rename a saved browser Playground, delete it with consequence copy, and open local-directory saved references
Operate the Site Manager drawer for settings, destructive reset or saved reload, file browsing and editing, Blueprint authoring, database tools, and logs
Browse, search, filter, inspect, copy, download, and run Blueprint gallery entries while keeping the selected Blueprint detail visible
Import .zip, run Blueprint URL, export to GitHub, download .zip, and download database.sqlite with import-over-current-site consequences shown
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
